name: RaspyJack
description: Raspberry Pi network-attack toolkit (Bash Bunny / LAN-turtle style) with an LCD HAT menu UI — MITM, responder, nmap, payloads. Installed via an upstream setup script on Raspberry Pi OS — NOT distributed as a prebuilt .img, so CC has no auto-resolved flashable image for it yet.
note: HONEST STATUS: RaspyJack is a git-clone + install-script overlay on Raspberry Pi OS, not an .img distribution — every upstream release ships zero image assets, so discover_sd_images('raspyjack') resolves to nothing and this profile cannot currently be imaged (owner decision pending: wire a real .img.xz asset path vs relabel vs remove from the matrix). id matches sd_backend.PI_IMAGE_PROFILES key 'raspyjack' (registry repo casing is 7h30th3r0n3/Raspyjack; GitHub resolves it case-insensitively). SD imaging requires an explicit removable target + confirmed=True.
